Initializing UIX

Initializing UIX is a straightforward process of creating the UIX engine and the font renderer and default plug-ins, registering the plug-ins, and enabling features your title uses.

Start by calling UIXCreateLiveEngine to get a pointer to the engine's interface. Your title must specify the skin file and language it uses when it creates the engine. UIXCreateLiveEngine passes the engine's interface pointer back to your title. It then uses the ILiveEngine interface pointer to call the engine's methods.

Note    Your title must create a Direct3D device (with IDirect3D8::CreateDevice) before calling UIXCreateLiveEngine.

After your title obtains the engine's interface pointer, it must register the plug-ins it uses. These plug-ins can be the default plug-ins or custom plug-ins implemented by the title. To register plug-ins, call ILiveEngine::SetAudioPlugin and ILiveEngine::SetUIPlugin. The font renderer plug-in ITitleFontRenderer, which must be implemented by the title, is passed as an argument to UIXCreateUIPlugin when it creates the UI plug-in.

Note    Obtain pointers to the default plug-ins by calling UIXCreateAudioPlugin and UIXCreateUIPlugin.

After initialization, Drop-in UI features can be started as needed. Any features that titles use must be enabled with ILiveEngine::EnableFeature prior to use. EnableFeature links the appropriate code in with the title but doesn't allocate any memory, so it is typically called at initialization time.

  • A Simple UIX Initialization Skeleton
  • Registering the Audio Plug-in
  • Registering the UI Plug-in

A Simple UIX Initialization Skeleton

The following code sample demonstrates the initialization of UIX.

#include <xtl.h>
#include <xonline.h>
#include <xact.h>
#include <uix.h>

// Xbox Drop-in UI interface pointers.
CTitleFontRenderer ctfr;
ITitleAudioPlugin *pAudioPlugin;
ITitleUIPlugin *pUIPlugin;
ILiveEngine *pLiveEngine;

// Simple title initialization
XOnlineStartup( NULL );
XInitDevices( 0, NULL );

// Create the Xbox Drop-in UI engine, the font plug-in, and the default audio and UI plug-ins.
ctfr.OpenFont( L"d:\\media\\arialuni.ttf" );
UIXCreateAudioPlugin( pXactEngine, pXactSoundBank, &pAudioPlugin );
UIXCreateUIPlugin( &ctfr, &pUIPlugin );
UIXCreateLiveEngine( "d:\\media\\default.uix", XC_LANGUAGE_ENGLISH, &pLiveEngine );

// Register the plug-ins and enable features the title uses.
pLiveEngine->SetUIPlugin( pUIPlugin );
pLiveEngine->SetAudioPlugin( pAudioPlugin );
pLiveEngine->EnableFeature( UIX_LOGON_FEATURE );
pLiveEngine->EnableFeature( UIX_FRIENDS_FEATURE );

This example uses the default font and skin files that are included with the Xbox SDK (redist\stats\media\ArialUni.Ttf and source\uix\default.inx). Use SkinBld to build the default.uix skin file from default.inx, the text skin description file.

After the example code executes, the engine is initialized and features can be started as needed.

Note    In this example, error checking has been removed to streamline flow. Most of the functions return an HRESULT value that should be checked with the SUCCEEDED (or similar) macro.

Registering the Audio Plug-in

The default audio plug-in requires that your title obtain pointers to the XACT engine and XACT sound bank so that it can use XACT to provide sound for the user interface. Create the XACT engine by calling XACTEngineCreate. You can use the XACT project, included with the Xbox SDK (in source\uix\UIXDefault.xap), to create default sound and wave banks. Choose Build from the Visual Studio File menu after opening the project. Alternatively, pre-built sound and wave banks are available in redist\uix\media. Register the wave bank by calling IXACTEngine::RegisterWaveBank. To register the sound bank, invoke IXACTEngine::CreateSoundBank. Pass the resulting pXactEngine and pXactSoundBank pointers to UIXCreateAudioPlugin to create the default audio plug-in. Titles can modify the default sound and wave banks, use their own banks, or provide a custom implementation of the audio plug-in to provide audio support through some other means.

The following example code provides a more specific view of XACT initialization. This example uses the default XACT sound and wave banks that are included with the Xbox SDK (in redist\uix\media).

PXACTWAVEBANK pXactWaveBank;
PXACTSOUNDBANK pXactSoundBank;
PXACTENGINE pXactEngine;

XACT_RUNTIME_PARAMETERS Params;
PVOID SoundBankData;
PVOID WaveBankData;
DWORD SoundBankSize;
DWORD WaveBankSize;

LoadFile( "d:\\media\\UIXDefault.xwb", &WaveBankData, &WaveBankSize );
LoadFile( "d:\\media\\UIXDefault.xsb", &SoundBankData, &SoundBankSize);

ZeroMemory( &Params, sizeof(Params) );
Params.dwMaxConcurrentStreams = 16;
Params.dwMax2DHwVoices = 40;
Params.dwMax3DHwVoices = 10;
XACTEngineCreate( &Params, &pXactEngine );

pXactEngine->RegisterWaveBank( WaveBankData, WaveBankSize, &pXactWaveBank );
pXactEngine->CreateSoundBank( SoundBankData, SoundBankSize, &pXactSoundBank );

The LoadFile() support function simply loads a file into memory:

BOOL LoadFile( LPSTR pszFile, PVOID *pvData, DWORD *pdwSize )
{
    BOOL fRes = FALSE;
    DWORD dwRead;
    HANDLE hFile = CreateFile( pszFile, GENERIC_READ, 0, NULL, OPEN_EXISTING, 0, NULL );
    if( INVALID_HANDLE_VALUE != hFile ) {
        *pdwSize = GetFileSize( hFile, NULL );	
        if( *pvData = HeapAlloc( GetProcessHeap(), 0, *pdwSize ) ) {
            fRes = ReadFile( hFile, *pvData, *pdwSize, &dwRead, NULL );		
        }	
        CloseHandle( hFile );
    }
    return fRes;
}

Registering the UI Plug-in

When registering the default UI plugin, UIXCreateUIPlugin requires an ITitleFontRenderer interface that is used to render text. The following code is a simple example implementation of ITitleFontRenderer using XFONT. The font renderer plug-in need not be implemented with XFONT; it can be implemented using the title's existing font support.

class CTitleFontRenderer : public ITitleFontRenderer
{
public:
    CTitleFontRenderer() { m_pXFont = NULL; };
    ~CTitleFontRenderer() { if( m_pXFont ) { m_pXFont->Release(); } };
    STDMETHODIMP_(ULONG)Release() { delete this; return 0; };
    STDMETHODIMP OpenFont( LPCWSTR pszFont ) {
        return XFONT_OpenTrueTypeFont( pszFont, 0x10000, &m_pXFont );
    };
    STDMETHODIMP DrawText( LPCWSTR pText, DWORD X, DWORD Y, DWORD MaxWidth ) {
        if( MaxWidth > 0 ) {
            D3DRECT r = { X, Y, X + MaxWidth, Y + 200 };
            m_pXFont->SetClippingRectangle( &r );
        }
        return m_pXFont->TextOut( g_pSurface, pText, -1, X, Y );
    };
    STDMETHODIMP GetTextSize( LPCWSTR pText, DWORD *pWidth, DWORD *pHeight ) {
        *pHeight = m_pXFont->GetTextHeight();
        return m_pXFont->GetTextExtent( pText, -1, (UINT *)pWidth );
    };
    STDMETHODIMP SetColor( D3DCOLOR Color ) {
        m_pXFont->SetTextColor( Color );
        return S_OK;
    };
    STDMETHODIMP SetHeight( DWORD Height ) {
        return m_pXFont->SetTextHeight( Height );
    };

private:
    XFONT *m_pXFont;
};
